title: ChaCha20-Poly1305 and Algorithm Choice · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Algorithm Agility Envelope
summary: A mobile product chooses an AEAD from benchmark folklore, mixes XChaCha and IETF nonce formats, and assumes changing algorithms repairs nonce reuse. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the algorithm agility envelope increment in portable AEAD selection and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Represent algorithm suite and parameters in an authenticated allow-listed envelope without negotiation downgrade.
Choose AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305 from platform acceleration, protocol requirements, misuse risk, compliance, interoperability, and measured performance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 8551 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to select and verify standard or XChaCha20-Poly1305 with exact sizes, AAD, vectors, and interoperability; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent algorithm suite and parameters in an authenticated allow-listed envelope without negotiation downgrade.
